posts.title: Codeforces Round #780 (Div. 3)
posts.text: 可惜没ak A Vasya decided to go to the grocery store. He found in his wallet coins of burle and coins of burles. He does not yet know the total cost of all goods, so help him find out (): the minimum positive integer amount of money he cannot pay without change or pay at all using only his coins. For example, if and (he has one 1-burle coin and one 2-burle coin), then: he can pay 1 burle without change, paying with one 1-burle coin, he can pay 2 burle without change, paying with one 2-burle coin, he can pay 3 burle without change by paying with one 1-burle coin and one 2-burle coin, he cannot pay 4 burle without change (moreover, he cannot pay this amount at all). So for and the answer is s=4. solution 显然 B Not so long ago, Vlad had a birthday, for which he was presented with a package of candies. There were types of candies, there are candies of the type (). Vlad decided to eat exactly one candy every time, choosing any of the candies of a type that is currently the most frequent (if there are several such types, he can choose any of them). To get the maximum pleasure from eating, Vlad does not want to eat two candies of the same type in a row. Help him figure out if he can eat all the candies without eating two identical candies in a row. solution 先对所有的从大到小排序。 时如果那么答案为NO 否则就为YES 时如果那么答案为YES否则为NO C A string is called even if it consists of a concatenation (joining) of strings of length consisting of the same characters. In other words, a string aaa is even if two conditions are satisfied at the same time: its length is even; for all odd (), is satisfied. For example, the following strings are even: "" (empty string), "tt", "aabb", "oooo", and "ttrrrroouuuuuuuukk". The following strings are not even: "aaa", "abab" and "abba". Given a string consisting of lowercase Latin letters. Find the minimum number of characters to remove from the string to make it even. The deleted characters do not have to be consecutive. solution 假设现在有一个子串为其中的各个字符各不相同 假设现在有一个字符在有相同的字符 考虑是否将与前面的字符配对。 如果配对那么删去，even string length max +2 如果不配对那么删去，其中 even string lenth max +2 可见不配对的情况一定不会比配对的情况更优 所以贪心能配对就配对并删除。 12345678910111213141516171819202122232425262728293031#include<bits/stdc++.h>using namespace std;const int maxn=2e5+5;bool pre[30];void solve(){ string s; cin>>s; int len=s.size(); int max_len=0; for(int i=0;i<26;i++)pre[i]=false; for(int i=0;i<len;i++) { if(pre[s[i]-'a']) { max_len+=2; for(int i=0;i<26;i++)pre[i]=false; } else pre[s[i]-'a']=true; } cout<<len-max_len<<endl; return;}int main(){ int t; cin>>t; while(t--)solve(); system("pause"); return 0;} D You are given an array consisting of integers. For each () the following inequality is true: . You can remove any number (possibly ) of elements from the beginning of the array and any number (possibly ) of elements from the end of the array. You are allowed to delete the whole array. You need to answer the question: how many elements should be removed from the beginning of the array, and how many elements should be removed from the end of the array, so that the result will be an array whose product (multiplication) of elements is maximal. If there is more than one way to get an array with the maximum product of elements on it, you are allowed to output any of them. The product of elements of an empty array (array of length ) should be assumed to be . solution 考虑让剩下的乘积最大 显然最后剩下的是一个非序列 所以我们通过分成若干个序列后分别讨论。 对于一个非序列 假设有个,个,个,个 倘若整个乘积为正数，那么这个序列最终的乘积就为 否则要删去一个负数。 考虑从前面删除还是后面删除即可。 123456789101112131415161718192021222324252627282930313233343536373839404142434445464748495051525354555657585960616263646566676869707172737475#include<bits/stdc++.h>using namespace std;const int maxn=2e5+5;int a[maxn];int Next[maxn];void solve(){ int n; cin>>n; for(int i=1;i<=n;i++)cin>>a[i],Next[i]=0; int Last=0; for(int i=1;i<=n;i++) { if(a[i]==0) { Next[Last]=i; Last=i; } } Next[Last]=n+1; Next[n+1]=0; int l=0,r=Next[l]; int ans_l=n,ans_r=0,ans=0; do { int x=0,y=0,c=0,d=0; for(int i=l+1;i<r;i++) { if(a[i]==1)x++; if(a[i]==2)y++; if(a[i]==-1)c++; if(a[i]==-2)d++; } int tmp=y+d,tmp_l=l,tmp_r=n-r+1; if((c+d)%2==0) { tmp=y+d; } else { int pos1=l+1,pos2=r-1,cost1=0,cost2=0; while(pos1<r&&a[pos1]>0)cost1+=a[pos1++]-1; cost1+=-1-a[pos1]; while(pos2>l&&a[pos2]>0)cost2+=a[pos2--]-1; cost2+=-1-a[pos2]; if(tmp-cost1>tmp-cost2) { tmp-=cost1; tmp_l=pos1; } else { tmp-=cost2; tmp_r=n-pos2+1; } } if(tmp>ans) { ans=tmp; ans_l=tmp_l; ans_r=tmp_r; } l=r; r=Next[r]; } while (r); cout<<ans_l<<" "<<ans_r<<endl;}int main(){ int t; cin>>t; while(t--)solve(); system("pause"); return 0;} E You are given a binary matrix of size . Rows are numbered from top to bottom from to , columns are numbered from left to right from to . The element located at the intersection of row and column is called . Consider a set of operations: Cyclically shift all rows up. The row with index will be written in place of the row (), the row with index will be written in place of the row . Cyclically shift all rows down. The row with index will be written in place of the row (), the row with index will be written in place of the row . Cyclically shift all columns to the left. The column with index will be written in place of the column (), the column with index will be written in place of the column . Cyclically shift all columns to the right. The column with index will be written in place of the column , the column with index will be written in place of the column . The matrix is shown on the left before the operation is applied to it, on the right — after. You can perform an arbitrary (possibly zero) number of operations on the matrix; the operations can be performed in any order. After that, you can perform an arbitrary (possibly zero) number of new xor-operations: Select any element and assign it with new value . In other words, the value of will have to be written into element . Each application of this xor-operation costs one burl. Note that the shift operations — are free. These operations can only be performed before xor-operations are performed. Output the minimum number of burles you would have to pay to make the matrix unitary. A unitary matrix is a matrix with ones on the main diagonal and the rest of its elements are zeros (that is, if and otherwise). solution 这四种操作都不会影响元素间的相对位置。 枚举不同的对角线即可 假设目前枚举的对角线中的数量为。 中的数量为 那么 123456789101112131415161718192021222324252627282930313233343536373839#include<bits/stdc++.h>using namespace std;const int maxn=2e3+5;int a[maxn][maxn];void solve(){ int n,cnt=0,cnt1=0; char c; cin>>n; for(int i=1;i<=n;i++) for(int j=1;j<=n;j++) { cin>>c; a[i][j]=c-'0'; cnt+=a[i][j]; } for(int i=1;i<=n;i++) { int x=1,y=i,tmp=0; for(int j=1;j<=n;j++) { x++; y++; if(x>n)x=1; if(y>n)y=1; tmp+=a[x][y]; } if(cnt1<tmp)cnt1=tmp; } cout<<cnt+n-cnt1*2<<endl;}int main(){ int t; cin>>t; while(t--)solve(); system("pause"); return 0;} F This is the easy version of Problem F. The only difference between the easy version and the hard version is the constraints. We will call a non-empty string balanced if it contains the same number of plus and minus signs. For example: strings "+--+" and "++-+--" are balanced, and strings "+--", "--" and "" are not balanced. We will call a string promising if the string can be made balanced by several (possibly zero) uses of the following operation: replace two adjacent minus signs with one plus sign. In particular, every balanced string is promising. However, the converse is not true: not every promising string is balanced. For example, the string "-+---" is promising, because you can replace two adjacent minuses with plus and get a balanced string "-++-", or get another balanced string "-+-+". How many non-empty substrings of the given string are promising? Each non-empty promising substring must be counted in the answer as many times as it occurs in string . Recall that a substring is a sequence of consecutive characters of the string. For example, for string "+-+" its substring are: "+-", "-+", "+", "+-+" (the string is a substring of itself) and some others. But the following strings are not its substring: "--", "++", "-++". solution 考虑一个子串其中有个和个 如果子串 是promising的 那么或者或者 显然满足 如果显然-的个数大于+的个数。 那么根据抽屉原理，至少有对可以被替换掉。而我们需要替换对。我们只需要满足即可。 的计算可以用前缀和快速处理。 整理得: 令 那么 用数据结构去维护,以的值作为划分依据。 每次考虑的时候去相应的数据结构里找不比它大的个数。 考虑树状数组。 123456789101112131415161718192021222324252627282930313233343536373839404142434445464748495051525354555657585960616263646566#include<bits/stdc++.h>using namespace std;const int maxn=2e5+5;int sum[maxn];class Fenwick{private: int *a,len; int lowbit(int x) { return x&(-x); }public: Fenwick(int n):len(n) { a=new int[n]; for(int i=0;i<n;i++)a[i]=0; } ~Fenwick() { delete[] a; } void insert(int x,int k) { for(x;x<len;x+=lowbit(x)) a[x]+=k; } int getsum(int x) { int ans=0; for(x;x;x-=lowbit(x)) ans+=a[x]; return ans; }};void solve(){ int f=0,n; cin>>n; string s; cin>>s; long long ans=0; Fenwick tree[3]{(2*n+5),(2*n+5),(2*n+5)}; tree[0].insert(n+1,1); for(int i=0;i<n;i++) { if(s[i]=='-')f++; else f--; int d=f%3; if(d<0)d+=3; ans+=tree[d].getsum(f+n+1); tree[d].insert(f+n+1,1); } cout<<ans<<endl;}int main(){ int t; cin>>t; while(t--)solve(); system("pause"); return 0;}

posts.title: Codeforces Round #779 (Div. 2)
posts.text: tmd,这波属实是掉大分 A Today, Marin is at a cosplay exhibition and is preparing for a group photoshoot! For the group picture, the cosplayers form a horizontal line. A group picture is considered beautiful if for every contiguous segment of at least cosplayers, the number of males does not exceed the number of females (obviously). Currently, the line has nnn cosplayers which can be described by a binary string . The cosplayer is male if and female if . To ensure that the line is beautiful, you can invite some additional cosplayers (possibly zero) to join the line at any position. You can't remove any cosplayer from the line. Marin wants to know the minimum number of cosplayers you need to invite so that the group picture of all the cosplayers is beautiful. She can't do this on her own, so she's asking you for help. Can you help her? solution 只需要保证两个male之前的female至少两个即可 123456789101112131415161718192021222324252627282930#include<bits/stdc++.h>using namespace std;const int maxn=100+5;int Next[maxn];int main(){ int t; cin>>t; while(t--) { int len; cin>>len; string s; cin>>s; int ans=0,Last=-1; for(int i=0;i<len;i++) { if(s[i]=='0') { if(Last>=0) { if(i-Last<=2)ans+=3-i+Last; } Last=i; } } cout<<ans<<endl; } return 0;} B Marin wants you to count number of permutations that are beautiful. A beautiful permutation of length is a permutation that has the following property: gcd is the greatest common divisor. solution 首先观察发现为奇数时 可严格证明 (但是我不会) 然后考虑让 让一个奇数配一个偶数 C Shinju loves permutations very much! Today, she has borrowed a permutation from Juju to play with. The cyclic shift of a permutation is a transformation on the permutation such that will now become . Let's define the power of permutation as the number of distinct elements in the prefix maximums array of the permutation. The prefix maximums array is the array of length such that . For example, the power of is since and there are distinct elements in . Unfortunately, Shinju has lost the permutation ppp! The only information she remembers is an array , where is the power of the cyclic shift of the permutation . She's also not confident that she remembers it correctly, so she wants to know if her memory is good enough. Given the array , determine if there exists a permutation that is consistent with . You do not have to construct the permutation . solution 可以旋转让 (为啥？) 考虑和的关系 如果换到前面的那么 否则 然后显然数组中只有一个 以上两点便是条件 (鬼才知道) 关于该题构造的方法 傻逼题目 D

posts.title: Educational Codeforces Round 125 (Rated for Div. 2) Solution
posts.text: 依旧未能完成4道题的突破 (bushi) A There's a chip in the point of the coordinate plane. In one operation, you can move the chip from some point to some point if the Euclidean distance between these two points is an integer (i.e. is integer). Your task is to determine the minimum number of operations required to move the chip from the point to the point . Input The first line contains a single integer — number of test cases. The single line of each test case contains two integers and ( — the coordinates of the destination point. Output For each test case, print one integer — the minimum number of operations required to move the chip from the point to the point solution 显然答案不会超过，因为每一个点一定能够水平+竖直两种方式到达。只需要判断是否能够一步到达即可。 B You are given four integers , , and . You should build a sequence where and for each you can choose: either or Your goal is to build such a sequence that for all and is maximum possible. solution 贪心让最大。 C You are given a bracket sequence consisting of nnn characters '(' and/or )'. You perform several operations with it. During one operation, you choose the shortest prefix of this string ( some amount of first characters of the string ) that is good and remove it from the string. The prefix is considered good if one of the following two conditions is satisfied: this prefix is a regular bracket sequence; this prefix is a palindrome of length at least two. A bracket sequence is called regular if it is possible to obtain a correct arithmetic expression by inserting characters '+' and '1' into this sequence. For example, sequences (())(), () and (()(())) are regular, while )(, (() and (()))( are not. The bracket sequence is called palindrome if it reads the same back and forth. For example, the bracket sequences )), (( and )(() are palindromes, while bracket sequences (), )( and ))( are not palindromes. You stop performing the operations when it's not possible to find a good prefix. Your task is to find the number of operations you will perform on the given string and the number of remaining characters in the string. You have to answer independent test cases. solution 显然如果第一个是'('的话，那么后面无论是')'(合法括号序列)还是'('(回文串)，一定会连着后面的一起删掉。 我们考虑第一个是')'的时候 由于他不能在合法序列当中被删除掉。所以我们要找回文串。 显然两个')'之间的序列一定是回文串(中间只有'(') 12345678910111213141516171819202122232425262728293031323334353637383940#include<bits/stdc++.h>using namespace std;const int maxn=5e5+5;char s[maxn];int main(){ int t,n; cin>>t; while(t--) { cin>>n; for(int i=1;i<=n;i++)cin>>s[i]; int index=1,ans=0; while(index<=n) { if(s[index]=='(') { if(index==n)break; index+=2; ans++; continue; } else { int tmp=index+1; while(tmp<=n&&s[tmp]!=')')tmp++; if(tmp<=n) { ans++; index=tmp+1; } else break; } } cout<<ans<<" "<<n-index+1<<endl; } return 0;} D Monocarp is playing a strategy game. In the game, he recruits a squad to fight monsters. Before each battle, Monocarp has coins to spend on his squad. Before each battle starts, his squad is empty. Monocarp chooses one type of units and recruits no more units of that type than he can recruit with coins. There are types of units. Every unit type has three parameters: — the cost of recruiting one unit of the type; — the damage that one unit of the type deals in a second; — the amount of health of one unit of the type. Monocarp has to face monsters. Every monster has two parameters: — the damage that the monster deals in a second; — the amount of health the monster has. Monocarp has to fight only the monster during the battle. He wants all his recruited units to stay alive. Both Monocarp's squad and the monster attack continuously (not once per second) and at the same time. Thus, Monocarp wins the battle if and only if his squad kills the monster strictly faster than the monster kills one of his units. The time is compared with no rounding. For each monster, Monocarp wants to know the minimum amount of coins he has to spend to kill that monster. If this amount is greater than , then report that it's impossible to kill that monster. solution 假设当前敌人的参数为 我方的兵种参数为 假设招募个 那么需要满足 移项得: 取最小的话 显然直接枚举复杂度上过不去。 我们发现与是正相关的关系 考虑表示代价为时的最大值 如何计算呢？ 我们对上式进行处理 而根据性质 所以我们有 移项得： 所以 分析下时间复杂度 枚举是 加上枚举是一定会爆的。 我们考虑枚举个数（因为有一部分cost的f值是一样的会重复） 这样对于任意一个 我们只需要枚举 次 求和时间复杂度为就可以过了。 注意到最多有 优化得 先对进行求前缀最大值 然后对二分查找 12345678910111213141516171819202122232425262728293031323334#include<bits/stdc++.h>using namespace std;const int maxm=1e6+5;long long f[maxm];long long hd[maxm];int main(){ int n,C,m; long long c,h,d; scanf("%d%d",&n,&C); for(int i=1;i<=n;i++) { scanf("%lld%lld%lld",&c,&d,&h); hd[c]=max(hd[c],h*d); } for(int i=1;i<=C;i++)if(hd[i]) { for(int k=C/i;k>=0;k--) f[k*i]=max(f[k*i],k*hd[i]-1); } for(int i=1;i<=C;i++)if(f[i]<f[i-1])f[i]=f[i-1]; scanf("%d",&m); for(int i=1;i<=m;i++) { scanf("%lld%lld",&d,&h); int k=lower_bound(f+1,f+1+C,h*d)-f; if(k==C+1)printf("-1 "); else printf("%d ",k); } return 0;} E In this problem, we will consider complete undirected graphs consisting of vertices with weighted edges. The weight of each edge is an integer from to . An undirected graph is considered beautiful if the sum of weights of all edges incident to vertex is equal to the weight of MST in the graph. MST is the minimum spanning tree — a tree consisting of edges of the graph, which connects all vertices and has the minimum sum of weights among all such trees; the weight of MST is the sum of weights of all edges in it. Calculate the number of complete beautiful graphs having exactly nnn vertices and the weights of edges from to . Since the answer might be large, print it modulo . solution 这是无相完全图 模拟kruskal求MST的过程。 显然号所连的边的权重是前小（允许有重复） 我们设表示当前号点所在连通块的最大边为,连通块大小为的方案 考虑现在选个点加入到连通块中，树边长度为 首先要从剩下的个点中选出来，他们的与的边权为 然后考虑先前的个点与新来的个点连边每条边可以选的边权有个 一共有条边 个点内部的连边也同理。

posts.title: Hello World
posts.text: Welcome to Hexo! This is your very first post. Check documentation for more info. If you get any problems when using Hexo, you can find the answer in troubleshooting or you can ask me on GitHub. Quick Start Create a new post 1$ hexo new "My New Post" More info: Writing Run server 1$ hexo server More info: Server Generate static files 1$ hexo generate More info: Generating Deploy to remote sites 1$ hexo deploy More info: Deployment
